COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Next-Generation Sequencing вЂ” Solid Tumor Panel

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Patient Name: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
Date of Birth: [REDACTED вЂ” Age 66 at time of procedure]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: Medical Oncology / Hepatobiliary Surgery
Specimen ID: [LAB-SURG-2024-PDAC-0413]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) surgical resection tissue
Procedure: Open pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with en bloc SMV segmental resection
Primary Site: Pancreatic head and uncinate process
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), post-neoadjuvant therapy
Pathologic Stage at Resection: ypT3 N1 M0 (AJCC 8th edition, Stage IIB)
Prior Therapy: Neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (6 cycles) в†’ FOLFIRI (2 cycles) в†’ concurrent chemoradiation (capecitabine + IMRT/SBRT, 54 Gy in 28 fractions)
Assay Platform: Comprehensive Genomic Profiling (CGP), 648-gene panel, DNA + RNA (tumor tissue);
Germline Testing: Separate peripheral blood germline assay performed (results incorporated below)
Sequencing Coverage: Mean tumor depth >500x; matched germline control sequenced
Tumor Cellularity Estimate: 40% (confirmed by pathology review, areas of treatment effect excluded)
Report Status: FINAL

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
QUALITY METRICS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
DNA Input: 200 ng (adequate)
RNA Input: Adequate for fusion analysis
Mapping Rate: 98.3%
On-target Rate: 97.1%
Uniform Coverage (>0.2x mean): 99.4% of targeted bases
Low-quality bases: <0.5%
Overall Sample Quality: PASS
Note: Specimen demonstrated expected treatment-related changes including fibrosis, calcifications, and necrosis (College of American Pathologists tumor regression grade 2 вЂ” moderate response). Macrodissection performed by pathologist to enrich for viable tumor regions. Results are considered interpretable and reliable within the context of moderate tumor cellularity.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SUMMARY OF FINDINGS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):       3 mutations/Mb  [LOW]
Microsatellite Status (MSI/MSS):     Microsatellite Stable (MSS)
Homologous Recombination Deficiency: Low (HRD score not elevated)
Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH):        Low (genome-wide LOH <10%)
HER2 Amplification:                  Not detected
PD-L1 Expression (IHC, 22C3):        Combined Positive Score (CPS) = 1  [PD-L1 Low / Negative]
NTRK1/2/3 Fusions:                   Not detected
RET Fusions:                          Not detected
MET Amplification/Exon 14 Skipping:  Not detected
BRCA1 (Somatic):                     No pathogenic variant detected
BRCA2 (Somatic):                     No pathogenic variant detected
BRCA1/2 (Germline, separate assay):  No pathogenic or likely pathogenic germline variant detected

Clinical Interpretation Summary:
This KRAS G12V-mutant, TP53-mutant, SMAD4-deleted, CDKN2A-deleted pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma is characterized by genomic alterations that represent the classic high-risk molecular profile of PDAC. The tumor is microsatellite stable with low TMB, rendering it unlikely to respond to immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy. No pathogenic BRCA1/2, ATM, or PALB2 alterations are identified (somatic or germline), precluding platinum- or PARP inhibitor-based targeted strategies based on homologous recombination deficiency. No currently actionable targets for FDA-approved targeted therapies are identified. SMAD4 deletion and CDKN2A homozygous deletion are consistent with an aggressive biological phenotype and historically associated with higher risk of systemic recurrence and shorter progression-free survival. Germline testing is negative, and no hereditary pancreatic cancer syndrome is identified.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
DETAILED GENOMIC FINDINGS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

1. KRAS p.G12V (c.35G>T) вЂ” Pathogenic / Tier I (Strong Clinical Significance)
   Gene: KRAS (Kirsten rat sarcoma viral proto-oncogene)
   Variant Classification: Missense mutation
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 42%
   Exon: 2, Codon 12
   Copy Number Context: Diploid (no amplification)
   Germline Correlation: Somatic (confirmed by matched normal)
   Functional Significance: KRAS G12V is a gain-of-function driver mutation resulting in constitutive activation of the KRAS GTPase. The glycine-to-valine substitution at codon 12 reduces GTPase activity, locking KRAS in the GTP-bound active state. This leads to sustained activation of downstream effectors including the RAF/MEK/ERK (MAPK) and PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathways, driving uncontrolled cellular proliferation, inhibition of apoptosis, and metabolic reprogramming. KRAS G12V is one of the most common oncogenic mutations in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, present in approximately 85-90% of PDAC cases, with G12D and G12V comprising the majority. The VAF of 42% is consistent with a heterozygous mutation in a sample with approximately 40% tumor cellularity, indicating clonal representation.
   Clinical Relevance: As of report generation, no FDA-approved KRAS G12V-specific inhibitor is available for pancreatic adenocarcinoma. KRAS G12C-specific inhibitors (sotorasib, adagrasib) do not bind the G12V variant due to the absence of the critical cysteine residue used for covalent attachment. Novel pan-KRAS, KRAS G12D, and KRAS G12V inhibitors are under active clinical investigation. Clinical trials enrolling KRAS-mutant PDAC patients should be considered. Combined MEK/ERK pathway inhibition strategies remain investigational in PDAC.

2. TP53 p.R175H (c.524G>A) вЂ” Pathogenic / Tier I (Strong Clinical Significance)
   Gene: TP53 (Tumor protein p53)
   Variant Classification: Missense mutation (gain-of-function hotspot)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 38%
   Exon: 5
   Copy Number Context: Diploid
   Germline Correlation: Somatic (confirmed by matched normal); Li-Fraumeni syndrome excluded by germline testing
   Functional Significance: TP53 R175H is among the most common and well-characterized gain-of-function TP53 mutations. Located within the DNA-binding domain, the arginine-to-histidine substitution at codon 175 disrupts the structural integrity of the p53 DNA-binding interface, abrogating wild-type tumor suppressor function. In addition, R175H-mutant p53 protein acquires oncogenic gain-of-function activities including promotion of invasion, genomic instability, resistance to apoptosis, and interaction with components of the transcriptional machinery (e.g., NF-Y, p63, p73). TP53 mutations are present in approximately 70-75% of PDAC cases and are associated with late-stage disease progression. The concordant VAF of ~38% alongside KRAS G12V (~42%) supports these as early, clonal driver events in this tumor.
   Clinical Relevance: No approved targeted therapy directly addresses mutant TP53 in PDAC. APR-246 (eprenetapopt) and other p53 reactivation agents remain investigational and have not shown activity in PDAC-specific trials. TP53 mutation status may have prognostic implications and is relevant to the tumor's overall biology and chemotherapy response. Presence of a gain-of-function TP53 hotspot mutation may influence resistance to certain chemotherapy agents.

3. SMAD4 вЂ” Homozygous Deletion вЂ” Pathogenic / Tier I (Prognostic Significance)
   Gene: SMAD4 (SMAD Family Member 4; also known as DPC4 вЂ” Deleted in Pancreatic Cancer locus 4)
   Variant Classification: Homozygous deletion (copy number loss, bi-allelic inactivation)
   Chromosomal Location: 18q21.2
   Copy Number: 0 (homozygous deletion confirmed)
   Germline Correlation: Somatic deletion; no germline SMAD4 pathogenic variant
   Functional Significance: SMAD4 encodes a critical mediator of the canonical TGF-ОІ/BMP signaling pathway. As a transcription factor and central hub of the SMAD signaling complex, SMAD4 transduces TGF-ОІ receptor signals to regulate cell growth arrest, differentiation, and apoptosis. Homozygous deletion of SMAD4 is identified in approximately 50-55% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas and represents loss of a major tumor suppressor. Loss of SMAD4 converts TGF-ОІ signaling from tumor-suppressive to tumor-promoting, enabling epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT), invasion, and enhanced metastatic potential. SMAD4 loss has been identified as a particularly important prognostic marker in resectable PDAC: multiple studies, including data from the RTOG 9704 trial correlative analysis, have demonstrated that SMAD4-negative tumors are associated with higher rates of distant metastatic recurrence compared to local recurrence, and with shorter overall survival following resection. This finding is clinically significant in the context of this patient's disease course.
   Clinical Relevance: SMAD4 loss may help stratify risk for systemic recurrence. Currently no targeted therapy directly addresses SMAD4 loss in PDAC. This alteration may have implications for patient stratification in clinical trials and for counseling regarding recurrence risk patterns.

4. CDKN2A/p16 вЂ” Homozygous Deletion вЂ” Pathogenic / Tier I (Driver Alteration)
   Gene: CDKN2A (Cyclin-Dependent Kinase Inhibitor 2A; also encodes p16-INK4A and ARF/p14)
   Variant Classification: Homozygous deletion (bi-allelic inactivation)
   Chromosomal Location: 9p21.3
   Copy Number: 0 (homozygous deletion confirmed; both p16 and ARF coding sequences lost)
   Germline Correlation: Somatic; no germline CDKN2A pathogenic variant identified (relevant given the patient's smoking history and Ghanaian ancestry; familial melanoma/pancreatic cancer syndrome excluded)
   Functional Significance: CDKN2A encodes two critical tumor suppressors via alternative reading frames: p16-INK4A, which inhibits CDK4/6-mediated phosphorylation of RB1 to prevent cell cycle progression at the G1/S checkpoint, and p14-ARF, which stabilizes p53 by sequestering MDM2. Homozygous CDKN2A deletion, present in >90% of PDAC tumors, leads to unrestrained CDK4/6 activity, hyperphosphorylation of RB1, and loss of cell cycle control. Combined with KRAS-driven proliferative signaling, CDKN2A loss creates a permissive cellular context for rapid tumor progression. The concurrent deletion of the ARF locus further compromises p53 pathway integrity, compounding the effect of the co-existing TP53 R175H gain-of-function mutation.
   Clinical Relevance: CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) have theoretical relevance in CDKN2A-deleted tumors, though clinical activity in PDAC has not been established in prospective trials. Enrollment into CDK4/6-based combination trials may be considered in the setting of disease progression.

5. ARID1A p.Q556* (c.1666C>T) вЂ” Likely Pathogenic / Tier II (Uncertain Therapeutic Significance)
   Gene: ARID1A (AT-Rich Interaction Domain 1A)
   Variant Classification: Nonsense (truncating) mutation
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 18%
   Exon: 8
   Copy Number Context: Heterozygous (one allele retained)
   Germline Correlation: Somatic
   Functional Significance: ARID1A encodes a subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex. Loss-of-function mutations in ARID1A impair nucleosome repositioning, disrupting transcriptional regulation of genes involved in differentiation, DNA damage repair, and cell cycle control. In PDAC, ARID1A mutations occur in approximately 6-10% of cases and are often subclonal (as suggested by the lower VAF of 18% compared to the clonal drivers KRAS and TP53). Subclonal ARID1A loss may contribute to intratumoral heterogeneity and treatment resistance. Of note, ARID1A-mutant tumors have shown synthetic lethality with PARP inhibitors and EZH2 inhibitors in preclinical models, and preliminary clinical data exist for certain tumor types. In pancreatic cancer, the clinical significance of isolated heterozygous ARID1A truncation without promoter hypermethylation or bi-allelic inactivation remains uncertain.
   Clinical Relevance: The subclonal nature and monoallelic status of this alteration limit immediate therapeutic actionability. This variant may be of interest for clinical trial enrollment in ARID1A/SWI/SNF-directed investigational studies.

6. KDM6A вЂ” Missense Variant of Uncertain Significance (VUS)
   Gene: KDM6A (Lysine Demethylase 6A; UTX)
   Variant Classification: Missense variant
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 9%
   Copy Number Context: Heterozygous
   Germline Correlation: Somatic
   Functional Significance: KDM6A encodes a histone H3K27 demethylase involved in epigenetic regulation of gene expression. KDM6A mutations have been described across multiple tumor types, and loss of KDM6A function can affect developmental transcription factor activity and chromatin accessibility. The low VAF (9%) is consistent with a late subclonal event or a variant in a minor subpopulation. This variant does not alter a known functional domain and lacks strong evidence for pathogenicity or driver function in PDAC based on current literature.
   Clinical Relevance: No established clinical significance at this time. Considered a variant of uncertain significance (VUS). Will be reclassified as additional evidence accrues.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
ALTERATIONS NOT DETECTED (KEY NEGATIVE FINDINGS)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
вЂў KRAS G12C: Not detected (relevant for KRAS G12C-specific inhibitor eligibility)
вЂў KRAS G12D: Not detected
вЂў BRCA1 (somatic): No pathogenic variant
вЂў BRCA2 (somatic): No pathogenic variant
вЂў BRCA1/2 (germline): No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant вЂ” germline hereditary breast-ovarian cancer syndrome excluded
вЂў ATM: No pathogenic mutation detected (clinically relevant for PARP inhibitor consideration)
вЂў PALB2: No pathogenic mutation detected
вЂў CHEK2: No pathogenic mutation detected
вЂў RAD51C/RAD51D: No pathogenic mutations detected
вЂў FANCONI pathway genes (FANC-A, -C, -D2, -E, -F, -G): No pathogenic alterations
вЂў HER2 (ERBB2): No amplification; no activating mutation
вЂў FGFR1/2/3/4: No amplifications or fusions detected
вЂў ALK: No rearrangement detected
вЂў ROS1: No rearrangement detected
вЂў RET: No rearrangement detected
вЂў NTRK1/2/3: No fusion detected (relevant for tumor-agnostic larotrectinib/entrectinib eligibility)
вЂў MET: No amplification; no exon 14 skipping mutation
вЂў PIK3CA: No hotspot mutation detected
вЂў PTEN: No deletion or pathogenic mutation detected
вЂў BRAF V600E: Not detected
вЂў IDH1/IDH2: No mutation detected
вЂў RB1: No pathogenic alteration (RB1 protein likely depleted functionally due to CDKN2A deletion; direct RB1 mutation absent)
вЂў mismatch repair genes (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2): No pathogenic mutations; MSI-H excluded
вЂў STK11: No pathogenic mutation
вЂў KEAP1/NF2: No pathogenic alterations detected

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
FUSION / STRUCTURAL VARIANT ANALYSIS (RNA-BASED)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Total fusions assessed: 397 gene pairs
Fusions detected: None clinically significant
NTRK1/2/3 fusions: Not detected
RET fusions: Not detected
ALK fusions: Not detected
ROS1 fusions: Not detected
FGFR2 fusions: Not detected
Note: RNA quality from the FFPE specimen was adequate for fusion analysis. No structural rearrangements with actionable therapeutic implications were identified.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB) AND MICROSATELLITE STATUS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
TMB: 3 mutations/Mb (Low; threshold for TMB-high: в‰Ґ10 mutations/Mb)
Microsatellite Instability (MSI): Microsatellite Stable (MSS)
MMR Protein IHC (performed on surgical resection specimen, reported separately): MLH1 intact, MSH2 intact, MSH6 intact, PMS2 intact вЂ” proficient MMR (pMMR)
Interpretation: The low TMB and MSS/pMMR status indicate this tumor is unlikely to respond to immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI) monotherapy. Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) has tumor-agnostic FDA approval for MSI-H/dMMR and TMB-high (в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb) tumors; this patient's tumor does not meet either criterion. The low TMB is consistent with the typical PDAC genomic landscape, which tends to be immunologically cold with limited tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes and a highly desmoplastic, immunosuppressive microenvironment.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION DEFICIENCY (HRD) AND GERMLINE ANALYSIS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
HRD Score: Low (Myriad myChoice-equivalent composite HRD score <42; genome-wide LOH <10%)
Genomic Scarring Signatures: Mutational signature analysis (COSMIC v3.3) reveals predominance of:
  вЂў Signature 1 (spontaneous cytosine deamination/aging): 31%
  вЂў Signature 5 (unknown etiology, common in PDAC): 28%
  вЂў Signature 3 (BRCA1/2-associated HRD): Not enriched (<5%)
  вЂў Signature 4 (tobacco/APOBEC): Low (8%), consistent with prior 15-pack-year smoking history
  вЂў Signature 6 (mismatch repair deficiency): Not detected
Interpretation: The absence of Signature 3 (HRD-associated) genomic scarring, combined with wild-type BRCA1/2, ATM, PALB2, and RAD51 pathway genes, indicates this tumor does not exhibit homologous recombination deficiency. Olaparib (Lynparza) and rucaparib (Rubraca), approved for germline BRCA-mutant metastatic PDAC maintenance therapy, are not indicated based on these findings.

Germline Testing Results (Peripheral Blood, Separate Assay вЂ” Final):
Panel: Hereditary Cancer Multi-Gene Panel (including BRCA1, BRCA2, ATM, PALB2, CDH1, STK11, PRSS1, SPINK1, CHEK2, RAD51C, RAD51D, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, APC, MUTYH, and others)
Result: NO pathogenic or likely pathogenic germline variant identified
Variants of Uncertain Significance (germline VUS): None reported
Interpretation: No hereditary pancreatic cancer syndrome identified. Familial PDAC, hereditary breast-ovarian cancer syndrome (HBOC), Lynch syndrome, Peutz-Jeghers syndrome, and familial atypical mole-malignant melanoma (FAMMM) syndrome are excluded on a genetic basis. Genetic counseling was performed; the patient and her family members do not require cascade testing based on these results.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
PD-L1 EXPRESSION
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Assay: PD-L1 IHC (22C3 pharmDx assay, Dako)
Combined Positive Score (CPS): 1
Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): <1%
Interpretation: PD-L1 expression is low/negative. PD-L1 low expression in combination with MSS/pMMR status indicates a low likelihood of response to anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 monotherapy. This is consistent with the characteristically immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment of PDAC, which features abundant cancer-associated fibroblasts, myeloid-derived suppressor cells (MDSCs), and regulatory T cells, with limited CD8+ T cell infiltration. Combination immune therapy approaches targeting the PDAC immunosuppressive microenvironment remain under investigation in clinical trials.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS (ADDITIONAL DETAIL)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
High-level amplifications (в‰Ґ8 copies): None detected
Focal deletions of note:
  вЂў SMAD4 (18q21.2): Homozygous deletion [CN=0] вЂ” confirmed driver
  вЂў CDKN2A/CDKN2B (9p21.3): Homozygous deletion [CN=0] вЂ” confirmed driver; CDKN2B (p15-INK4B) co-deleted
Arm-level copy number changes:
  вЂў 17p loss (TP53 locus): Consistent with LOH at TP53, supporting bi-allelic inactivation in concert with the R175H point mutation
  вЂў 18q loss (SMAD4 locus): Broad 18q arm deletion confirmed; SMAD4 homozygous deletion superimposed on background arm-level loss
  вЂў 9p loss: Arm-level 9p loss underlying CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion
  вЂў Overall chromosomal instability: Moderate; consistent with PDAC genomic landscape
Note: No amplification of ERBB2, MYC, CDK4, CDK6, CCND1, or CCND2 identified.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
CLINICAL INTERPRETATION AND THERAPEUTIC IMPLICATIONS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
This comprehensive genomic profile is consistent with the canonical molecular subtype of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, characterized by the co-occurrence of KRAS activating mutation, TP53 gain-of-function mutation, CDKN2A homozygous deletion, and SMAD4 homozygous deletion вЂ” the four most common driver alterations in PDAC (the so-called "PDAC quadruplet"). This molecular combination is associated with:

1. Highly aggressive tumor biology with elevated risk of systemic recurrence following surgical resection.
2. Lack of currently actionable targets for FDA-approved molecularly targeted therapies.
3. SMAD4 loss specifically identifying a subset of patients at higher risk for distant (hepatic/peritoneal) rather than local recurrence вЂ” a clinically relevant consideration in planning adjuvant therapy and surveillance.
4. Microsatellite stable status precluding eligibility for pembrolizumab under tumor-agnostic MSI-H/dMMR indication.
5. Low TMB precluding eligibility for pembrolizumab under tumor-agnostic TMB-H indication.
6. Wild-type BRCA1/2 and absence of HRD precluding olaparib maintenance therapy.
7. Absence of NTRK fusions precluding larotrectinib/entrectinib eligibility.

Recommended Actions (for oncology team consideration):
вЂў Standard adjuvant chemotherapy as planned per institutional and trial-based protocols (note: this report was finalized following resection; adjuvant therapy decision to be made by treating oncologist).
вЂў Enrollment in clinical trials targeting KRAS G12V (selective KRAS inhibitors, pan-KRAS degraders), PDAC immunotherapy combinations, or SWI/SNF-directed therapy (given ARID1A mutation) should be considered at the time of any disease recurrence or progression.
вЂў Given SMAD4 loss, intensified surveillance for hepatic and peritoneal recurrence is clinically prudent.
вЂў Genetic counseling completed; no familial implications identified.
вЂў The ARID1A subclonal truncation mutation warrants attention at time of disease recurrence, as re-biopsy may demonstrate clonal evolution with therapeutic implications.

Note: This report reflects the genomic landscape of the primary pancreatic tumor at the time of surgical resection following neoadjuvant therapy. Clonal evolution may occur at recurrence; re-biopsy at the time of metastatic progression is strongly recommended to assess for acquisition of new actionable alterations.

This report was reviewed and interpreted by a board-certified molecular pathologist with expertise in oncologic genomics. The genomic findings are correlated with the morphologic diagnosis of moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (ypT3 N1 M0) following neoadjuvant therapy.
